title: Multi-module package (executable-verified, capability)
goal: Build a small Python package, verified by importing and running it.
prompt:
Create a Python package `tasklib/` at the project root with:
1. tasklib/__init__.py (may be empty)
2. tasklib/models.py: a `Task` dataclass with fields id:int, title:str, done:bool
3. tasklib/store.py: class `TaskStore` with add(title)->Task (auto-increment id, done=False), complete(id)->bool, list_all()->list[Task]
4. tasklib/cli.py: a `main()` function with an argparse CLI exposing subcommands `add <title>`, `done <id>`, `list` (printing to stdout)
5. tests/test_store.py: pytest tests for TaskStore
All imports must work and the tests must pass.
reference_solution: tasklib/ package (models, store, cli, main) + passing tests.

verify.cmd: python3 -c "from tasklib.models import Task; from tasklib.store import TaskStore; from tasklib.cli import main"
python3 -c "from tasklib.store import TaskStore; s=TaskStore(); t=s.add('x'); assert t.title=='x'; assert len(s.list_all())==1"
python3 -c "from tasklib.store import TaskStore; s=TaskStore(); t=s.add('x'); assert s.complete(t.id); assert s.list_all()[0].done"
